nativeRange.habitat: Shallow tropical reef flats, sandy substrates, and rocky areas in the photic zone; tends to form dense low mats
nativeRange.biotope: Marine benthic; anchors via rhizoids (holdfasts) into sandbed or rockwork in sheltered coastal environments

imageCandidates.notes: Robin White; 2048x1536. Tack-sharp macro filling the frame with the diagnostic tight whorled pom-pom tufts of fine branchlets radiating around upright stalks; vibrant live green, single-subject dominant.
Thomas Irvine; 886x886. Clean sharp macro of the fuzzy whorled ball-tufts on a live specimen; minimal background clutter, good coloration.
Robin White; 2048x1536. Sharp in-situ shot showing the growth habit of the green whorled tufts spreading over reef substrate; some red algae in background.

summary: Fuzzy Caulerpa (Caulerpa verticillata) is a compact, fast-growing saltwater macroalgae prized for the soft, fuzzy texture created by fine branchlets radiating in tight whorls around upright stalks. Beginner-friendly and suitable for display refugiums, though it shares the allelopathic and reproductive-crash risks common to all Caulerpa species.
careNotes:
Fuzzy Caulerpa (Caulerpa verticillata) is a siphonous green macroalgae prized for its unusual whorled morphology: short, fine branchlets radiate in tight whorls around upright stalks, creating a soft, fuzzy or feathery texture that sets it apart from other members of the genus. It remains a specialty item with limited trade availability, found primarily through specialty online macroalgae retailers.

ALLELOPATHY: Like all Caulerpa species, C. verticillata is expected to produce secondary metabolites (caulerpenyne, caulerpin, and sesquiterpenes) as chemical defenses. These compounds have been shown in research settings to inhibit coral growth and stress competing algae when released into the water column. Physical separation from sensitive SPS corals and use of activated carbon in the system are generally considered advisable precautions.

GOING SEXUAL / REPRODUCTIVE CRASH: All Caulerpa species can undergo holocarpy — a mass reproductive event in which the organism converts its cytoplasm into gametes and effectively dies. This can release large quantities of organic matter and trigger an acute water-quality event (ammonia spike, oxygen depletion) capable of crashing a reef tank. Triggers include sharp drops in nutrients, disrupted photoperiod, or rapid temperature swings. Mitigations: run refugium lighting on a 24-hour cycle to suppress reproductive triggering; harvest regularly to keep the colony in active vegetative growth; maintain stable (not zero) nutrient levels; remove any specimen showing reproductive structures (white/translucent fronds, turbid release) immediately.

INVASIVE CONCERN: This species is considered potentially invasive and is completely banned for shipment to California. Captive specimens must never be released into natural waterways.

GENERAL CARE: High lighting (5,000K or higher spectrum) and moderate water flow are generally considered appropriate, consistent with standard Caulerpa husbandry. Stable reef water parameters (NSW-level salinity 1.023–1.025, consistent temperature 22–26°C) are recommended. This species is reported to be prolifically-growing, and regular harvesting helps maintain vigorous vegetative growth while suppressing reproductive triggering.

For comparison, [caulerpa-prolifera] covers the Atlantic blade-form relative; [sea-grapes] covers the grape-cluster display Caulerpa; and [cactus-caulerpa] covers the cactus-like form. All share the allelopathy, reproductive-crash risk, and invasive-species caveats described above.
breedingNotes: Propagates in the aquarium via fragmentation — small pieces of stolon or frond have been reported to establish as new plants — and by vegetative tip growth along the stolon.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby trade source for this species. Provided growth form description, 'prolifically-growing' characterization, display placement, invasive status (banned in California), shipping/color-recovery notes, and general livestock compatibility.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Caulerpa verticillata
additional.notes: Taxonomy confirmed: Family Caulerpaceae, Order Bryopsidales, Kingdom Plantae, Division Chlorophyta. Distribution: warmer waters of Caribbean, Indian Ocean, Pacific Ocean, and Western Australia (Kimberley/Pilbara coast). Morphology: dense low mats spreading to ~10 cm; whorled branchlet growth form confirmed.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
